Details: Chlorine is very commonly used as a pre-treatment for arsenic removal. If As(III) is present, the water is chlorinated in order to oxidize As(III) to As(V). Removal efficiencies of most arsenic treatment processes are higher with As(V) than with As(III). Greater than 95 percent of As(III) was oxidized to As(V) with the application of chlorine for both ground waters and surface waters.
